title: hermodactylus tuberosus subsp. longifolius
intro: Hermodactylus tuberosus subsp. longifolius, also known as the autumn gentian, is a plant species within the Gentianaceae family, characterized by its yellow flowers and tuberous roots. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits associated with Hermodactylus tuberosus subsp. longifolius include its potential to treat fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems, as well as its ability to boost the immune system. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as tinctures, teas, and infusions, to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
